name: flask-mailman

metadata.name: Flask-Mailman
metadata.summary: Porting Django's email implementation to your Flask applications.

documentation:
# Flask-Mailman

![PyPI](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/flask-mailman?color=blue)
![PyPI - Downloads](https://img.shields.io/pypi/dm/flask-mailman?color=brightgreen)
[![dev workflow](https://github.com/waynerv/flask-mailman/actions/workflows/dev.yml/badge.svg?branch=master)](https://github.com/waynerv/flask-mailman/actions/workflows/dev.yml)
![GitHub commits since latest release (by SemVer)](https://img.shields.io/github/commits-since/waynerv/flask-mailman/latest?color=cyan)
![PyPI - License](https://img.shields.io/pypi/l/flask-mailman?color=blue)
[![codecov](https://codecov.io/gh/waynerv/flask-mailman/graph/badge.svg?token=bCkkuXW4dX)](https://codecov.io/gh/waynerv/flask-mailman)

Flask-Mailman is a Flask extension providing simple email sending capabilities.

It was meant to replace unmaintained Flask-Mail with a better warranty and more features.

## Usage

Flask-Mail ported Django's email implementation to your Flask applications, which may be the best mail sending implementation that's available for python.

The way of using this extension is almost the same as Django.

Documentation: https://waynerv.github.io/flask-mailman.

**Note: A few breaking changes have been made in v0.2.0 version** to ensure that API of this extension is basically the same as Django.
Users migrating from Flask-Mail should upgrade with caution.

## Credits

Thanks to [Jetbrains](https://jb.gg/OpenSource) for providing an Open Source license for this project.

[![Jetbrains Logo](docs/img/jetbrains-variant-4.png)](www.jetbrains.com)

Build tools and workflows of this project was inspired by [waynerv/cookiecutter-pypackage](https://github.com/waynerv/cookiecutter-pypackage) project template.

modules.name: flask_mailman.utils
documentation: Email message and email sending related helper functions.

functions.name: flask_mailman.utils.is_protected_type
documentation:
Determine if the object instance is of a protected type.

Objects of protected types are preserved as-is when passed to
force_text(strings_only=True).

functions.name: flask_mailman.utils.force_str
documentation:
Similar to smart_str(), except that lazy instances are resolved to
strings, rather than kept as lazy objects.

If strings_only is True, don't convert (some) non-string-like objects.

functions.name: flask_mailman.utils.punycode
documentation: Return the Punycode of the given domain if it's non-ASCII.

classes.name: flask_mailman.utils.CachedDnsName
documentation: Cache the hostname, but do it lazily: socket.getfqdn() can take a couple of
seconds, which slows down the restart of the server.

modules.name: flask_mailman.backends.dummy
documentation: Dummy email backend that does nothing.

modules.name: flask_mailman.backends.console
documentation: Email backend that writes messages to console instead of sending them.

methods.name: flask_mailman.backends.console.EmailBackend.send_messages

methods.name: flask_mailman.backends.smtp.EmailBackend.open
documentation:
Ensure an open connection to the email server. Return whether or not a
new connection was required (True or False) or None if an exception
passed silently.

methods.name: flask_mailman.backends.smtp.EmailBackend.close
documentation: Close the connection to the email server.

methods.name: flask_mailman.backends.smtp.EmailBackend.send_messages
documentation:
Send one or more EmailMessage objects and return the number of email
messages sent.

methods.name: flask_mailman.backends.smtp.EmailBackend._send
documentation: A helper method that does the actual sending.
A wrapper that manages the SMTP network connection.

modules.name: flask_mailman.backends.locmem
documentation: Backend for test environment.

classes.name: flask_mailman.backends.locmem.EmailBackend

methods.name: flask_mailman.backends.locmem.EmailBackend.send_messages
documentation: Redirect messages to the dummy outbox

An email backend for use during test sessions.

The test connection stores email messages in a dummy outbox,
rather than sending them out on the wire.

The dummy outbox is accessible through the outbox instance attribute.

modules.name: flask_mailman.backends
documentation: Mail backends shipped with Django.

modules.name: flask_mailman.backends.file
documentation: Email backend that writes messages to a file.

classes.name: flask_mailman.backends.file.ImproperlyConfigured
documentation: Application is somehow improperly configured

methods.name: flask_mailman.backends.file.EmailBackend._get_filename
documentation: Return a unique file name.

modules.name: flask_mailman.backends.base
documentation: Base email backend class.

classes.name: flask_mailman.backends.base.BaseEmailBackend

methods.name: flask_mailman.backends.base.BaseEmailBackend.open
documentation:
Open a network connection.

This method can be overwritten by backend implementations to
open a network connection.

It's up to the backend implementation to track the status of
a network connection if it's needed by the backend.

This method can be called by applications to force a single
network connection to be used when sending mails. See the
send_messages() method of the SMTP backend for a reference
implementation.

The default implementation does nothing.

methods.name: flask_mailman.backends.base.BaseEmailBackend.close
documentation: Close a network connection.

methods.name: flask_mailman.backends.base.BaseEmailBackend.send_messages
documentation:
Send one or more EmailMessage objects and return the number of email
messages sent.

Base class for email backend implementations.

Subclasses must at least overwrite send_messages().

open() and close() can be called indirectly by using a backend object as a
context manager:

   with backend as connection:
       # do something with connection
       pass

modules.name: flask_mailman
documentation: Tools for sending email.

methods.name: flask_mailman._MailMixin._get_backend_from_module
documentation:
import the backend module and return the backend class.

```
param backend_module_name

the string based module name from where the backend class will be imported.

param backend_class_name

the string based backend class name.
```

methods.name: flask_mailman._MailMixin.import_backend
documentation:
This is the base method to import the backend service.
This method will implement the feature for flask_mailman to take custom backends.

Now you can create your own backend class and implement with flask mailman.

```
for example:

from flask import Flask

app = Flask(__name__)
app.config['MAIL_BACKEND'] = 'locmem'
#or
app.config['MAIL_BACKEND'] = 'smtp'
#or
app.config['MAIL_BACKEND'] = 'flask_mailman.backends.locmem'
#or
app.config['MAIL_BACKEND'] = 'flask_mailman.backends.locmem.EmailBackend'
#or
app.config['MAIL_BACKEND'] = 'your_project.mail.backends.custom.EmailBackend'
```

methods.name: flask_mailman._MailMixin.get_connection
documentation:
Load an email backend and return an instance of it.

If backend is None (default), use app.config.MAIL_BACKEND.

Both fail_silently and other keyword arguments are used in the
constructor of the backend.

methods.name: flask_mailman._MailMixin.send_mail
documentation:
Easy wrapper for sending a single message to a recipient list. All members
of the recipient list will see the other recipients in the 'To' field.

If auth_user is None, use the MAIL_USERNAME setting.
If auth_password is None, use the MAIL_PASSWORD setting.

methods.name: flask_mailman._MailMixin.send_mass_mail
documentation:
Given a datatuple of (subject, message, from_email, recipient_list), send
each message to each recipient list. Return the number of emails sent.

If from_email is None, use the MAIL_DEFAULT_SENDER setting.
If auth_user and auth_password are set, use them to log in.
If auth_user is None, use the MAIL_USERNAME setting.
If auth_password is None, use the MAIL_PASSWORD setting.

Note: The API for this method is frozen. New code wanting to extend the
functionality should use the EmailMessage class directly.

classes.name: flask_mailman._Mail

at configuration time.

```
param app

Flask application instance
```

Manages email messaging

```
param app

Flask instance
```

functions.name: flask_mailman.message.forbid_multi_line_headers
documentation: Forbid multi-line headers to prevent header injection.

functions.name: flask_mailman.message.sanitize_address
documentation: Format a pair of (name, address) or an email address string.

variables.name: flask_mailman.message.utf8_charset.body_encoding
documentation: Python defaults to BASE64

methods.name: flask_mailman.message.MIMEMixin.as_string
documentation:
Return the entire formatted message as a string.
Optional `unixfrom' when True, means include the Unix From_ envelope
header.

This overrides the default as_string() implementation to not mangle
lines that begin with 'From '. See bug #13433 for details.

methods.name: flask_mailman.message.MIMEMixin.as_bytes
documentation:
Return the entire formatted message as bytes.
Optional `unixfrom' when True, means include the Unix From_ envelope
header.

This overrides the default as_bytes() implementation to not mangle
lines that begin with 'From '. See bug #13433 for details.

classes.name: flask_mailman.message.EmailMessage

methods.name: flask_mailman.message.EmailMessage.__init__
documentation:
Initialize a single email message (which can be sent to multiple
recipients).

methods.name: flask_mailman.message.EmailMessage.recipients
documentation:
Return a list of all recipients of the email (includes direct
addressees as well as Cc and Bcc entries).

methods.name: flask_mailman.message.EmailMessage.send
documentation: Send the email message.

methods.name: flask_mailman.message.EmailMessage.attach
documentation:
Attach a file with the given filename and content. The filename can
be omitted and the mimetype is guessed, if not provided.

If the first parameter is a MIMEBase subclass, insert it directly
into the resulting message attachments.

For a text/* mimetype (guessed or specified), when a bytes object is
specified as content, decode it as UTF-8. If that fails, set the
mimetype to DEFAULT_ATTACHMENT_MIME_TYPE and don't decode the content.

methods.name: flask_mailman.message.EmailMessage.attach_file
documentation:
Attach a file from the filesystem.

Set the mimetype to DEFAULT_ATTACHMENT_MIME_TYPE if it isn't specified
and cannot be guessed.

For a text/* mimetype (guessed or specified), decode the file's content
as UTF-8. If that fails, set the mimetype to
DEFAULT_ATTACHMENT_MIME_TYPE and don't decode the content.

methods.name: flask_mailman.message.EmailMessage._create_mime_attachment
documentation:
Convert the content, mimetype pair into a MIME attachment object.

If the mimetype is message/rfc822, content may be an
email.Message or EmailMessage object, as well as a str.

methods.name: flask_mailman.message.EmailMessage._create_attachment
documentation:
Convert the filename, content, mimetype triple into a MIME attachment
object.

methods.name: flask_mailman.message.EmailMessage._set_list_header_if_not_empty
documentation:
Set msg's header, either from self.extra_headers, if present, or from
the values argument.

class_variables.name: flask_mailman.message.EmailMessage.encoding
documentation: None => use settings default
A container for email information.

classes.name: flask_mailman.message.EmailMultiAlternatives

methods.name: flask_mailman.message.EmailMultiAlternatives.__init__
documentation:
Initialize a single email message (which can be sent to multiple
recipients).

methods.name: flask_mailman.message.EmailMultiAlternatives.attach_alternative
documentation: Attach an alternative content representation.

A version of EmailMessage that makes it easy to send multipart/alternative
messages. For example, including text and HTML versions of the text is
made easier.
